section: Preparing to install OpenShift GitOps
evidence: Read the following sizing requirements before you install OpenShift GitOps on OpenShift Container Platform
notes: Planning job that must be completed before installation

user_story: Understand resource requirements for default GitOps workloads
section: Sizing requirements for GitOps
evidence: The following table details the resource requests and limits for the default workloads
notes: Table shows CPU and memory requirements for 7 different GitOps components

user_story: Understand resource requirements for default GitOps workloads
task: Review default resource requests and limits
section: Sizing requirements for GitOps
evidence: The following table details the resource requests and limits for the default workloads
notes: Includes application-controller, applicationset-controller, server, repo-server, redis, dex-server, and redis-ha-haproxy

user_story: Understand resource requirements for default GitOps workloads
task: Check and modify ArgoCD custom resource specifications
section: Sizing requirements for GitOps
evidence: you can also use the ArgoCD custom resource with the `oc` command to see the specifics and modify them
notes: Use 'oc edit argocd <name of argo cd> -n namespace' to view and modify

user_story: Size Redis resources for large-scale deployments
section: Sizing requirements for Argo CD redis
evidence: During the capacity planning stage for your application in the OpenShift GitOps Operator, you must ensure that an adequate amount of resources, such as memory, CPU, and storage, are allocated for the argocd-redis pod
notes: Default memory limit may not be enough for large number of resources

user_story: Size Redis resources for large-scale deployments
section: Sizing requirements for Argo CD redis
evidence: oc get argocd -n openshift-gitops openshift-gitops -o json | jq '.spec.redis.resources'
notes: Shows current limits and requests for Redis pod

user_story: Size Redis resources for large-scale deployments
task: Adjust Redis memory limits for scale
section: Sizing requirements for Argo CD redis
evidence: oc patch argocd -n openshift-gitops openshift-gitops --type json -p '[{"op": "replace", "path": "/spec/redis/resources/limits/memory", "value": "8Gi"}...]'
notes: Example sets highest threshold to 8Gi and lowest to 256Mi

main_job: Install GitOps Operator via web console
section: Installing OpenShift GitOps
evidence: This guide explains how to install the OpenShift GitOps Operator to an OpenShift Container Platform cluster
notes: GUI-based installation workflow

main_job: Install GitOps Operator via web console
user_story: Navigate to OperatorHub and locate GitOps Operator
section: Installing OpenShift GitOps Operator in web console
evidence: Open the Administrator perspective of the web console and go to Operators -> OperatorHub
notes: First step in web console installation

main_job: Install GitOps Operator via web console
user_story: Navigate to OperatorHub and locate GitOps Operator
task: Search for OpenShift GitOps in OperatorHub
section: Installing OpenShift GitOps Operator in web console
evidence: Search for `OpenShift GitOps`, click the *OpenShift GitOps* tile, and then click *Install*
notes: Simple search and click workflow

main_job: Install GitOps Operator via web console
section: Installing OpenShift GitOps Operator in web console
evidence: On the Install Operator page: Select an Update channel, Select a GitOps Version to install, Choose an Installed Namespace
notes: Critical configuration choices that affect deployment

main_job: Install GitOps Operator via web console
section: Installing OpenShift GitOps Operator in web console
evidence: Select an Update channel. Select a GitOps Version to install
notes: Latest channel is default; version-specific channels available (e.g., gitops-1.19)

main_job: Install GitOps Operator via web console
section: Installing OpenShift GitOps Operator in web console
evidence: Choose an Installed Namespace. The default installation namespace is `openshift-gitops-operator`
notes: Default changed from openshift-operators to openshift-gitops-operator in v1.10+

main_job: Install GitOps Operator via web console
section: Installing OpenShift GitOps Operator in web console
evidence: Select the Enable Operator recommended cluster monitoring on this Namespace checkbox to enable cluster monitoring
notes: Can also enable via label: oc label namespace <namespace> openshift.io/cluster-monitoring=true

main_job: Install GitOps Operator via web console
section: Installing OpenShift GitOps Operator in web console
evidence: Verify that the OpenShift GitOps Operator is listed in Operators -> Installed Operators. The Status should resolve to Succeeded
notes: Includes automatic Argo CD instance setup in openshift-gitops namespace

main_job: Install GitOps Operator via web console
section: Installing OpenShift GitOps Operator in web console
evidence: Click Install to make the GitOps Operator available on the OpenShift Container Platform cluster
notes: GitOps is installed in all namespaces of the cluster

main_job: Install GitOps Operator via web console
section: Installing OpenShift GitOps Operator in web console
evidence: Verify that the OpenShift GitOps Operator is listed in Operators -> Installed Operators. The Status should resolve to Succeeded
notes: Also creates ready-to-use Argo CD instance and console icon

section: Installing OpenShift GitOps
evidence: You can install OpenShift GitOps Operator from the OperatorHub by using the CLI
notes: Command-line based installation workflow for automation

section: Installing OpenShift GitOps Operator using CLI
evidence: Create a `openshift-gitops-operator` namespace
notes: Namespace must exist before OperatorGroup and Subscription

section: Installing OpenShift GitOps Operator using CLI
evidence: oc create ns openshift-gitops-operator
notes: Default namespace changed from openshift-operators in v1.10+

section: Installing OpenShift GitOps Operator using CLI
evidence: oc label namespace <namespace> openshift.io/cluster-monitoring=true
notes: Optional step to enable monitoring

section: Installing OpenShift GitOps Operator using CLI
evidence: Create a OperatorGroup object YAML file, for example, gitops-operator-group.yaml
notes: OperatorGroup defines the scope and upgrade strategy

section: Installing OpenShift GitOps Operator using CLI
evidence: Example OperatorGroup: apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1, kind: OperatorGroup
notes: Sets upgradeStrategy to Default

section: Installing OpenShift GitOps Operator using CLI
evidence: oc apply -f gitops-operator-group.yaml
notes: Creates OperatorGroup resource in openshift-gitops-operator namespace

section: Installing OpenShift GitOps Operator using CLI
evidence: Create a Subscription object YAML file to subscribe a namespace to the OpenShift GitOps Operator
notes: Subscription triggers Operator installation from catalog

section: Installing OpenShift GitOps Operator using CLI
evidence: Example Subscription: channel: latest, installPlanApproval: Automatic, source: redhat-operators
notes: Key fields: channel (latest), source (redhat-operators), sourceNamespace (openshift-marketplace)

section: Installing OpenShift GitOps Operator using CLI
evidence: oc apply -f openshift-gitops-sub.yaml
notes: Triggers automatic installation based on installPlanApproval: Automatic

section: Installing OpenShift GitOps Operator using CLI
evidence: After the installation is complete, verify that all the pods in the openshift-gitops namespace are running
notes: Two namespaces to check: openshift-gitops (runtime) and openshift-gitops-operator (controller)

section: Installing OpenShift GitOps Operator using CLI
evidence: oc get pods -n openshift-gitops
notes: Should show 8 running pods: cluster, gitops-plugin, application-controller, applicationset-controller, dex-server, redis, repo-server, server

section: Installing OpenShift GitOps Operator using CLI
evidence: oc get pods -n openshift-gitops-operator
notes: Should show openshift-gitops-operator-controller-manager with 2/2 Ready

section: Logging in to the Argo CD instance by using the Argo CD admin account
evidence: Use the Argo CD admin account to log in to the default ready-to-use Argo CD instance or the newly installed and deployed Argo CD instance
notes: Post-installation access and authentication

section: Logging in to the Argo CD instance by using the Argo CD admin account
evidence: Navigate to the menu -> OpenShift GitOps -> Cluster Argo CD. The login page of the Argo CD UI is displayed in a new window
notes: Access via OpenShift console menu

task: Verify GitOps Operator installation in console
section: Logging in to the Argo CD instance by using the Argo CD admin account
evidence: In the Administrator perspective of the web console, navigate to Operators -> Installed Operators to verify that the OpenShift GitOps Operator is installed
notes: Pre-check before accessing Argo CD

task: Open Argo CD from console menu
section: Logging in to the Argo CD instance by using the Argo CD admin account
evidence: Navigate to the menu -> OpenShift GitOps -> Cluster Argo CD
notes: Opens Argo CD login page in new window

section: Logging in to the Argo CD instance by using the Argo CD admin account
evidence: Use `admin` as the Username and the copied password as the Password to log in to the Argo CD UI
notes: Two auth options: OpenShift SSO or admin credentials

section: Logging in to the Argo CD instance by using the Argo CD admin account
evidence: Use the navigation panel to go to the Workloads -> Secrets page. Select the <argo_CD_instance_name>-cluster instance to display the password. On the Details tab, copy the password under Data -> admin.password
notes: Password stored in argocd-secret managed by operator

task: Log in with admin username and password
section: Logging in to the Argo CD instance by using the Argo CD admin account
evidence: Use `admin` as the Username and the copied password as the Password to log in to the Argo CD UI in the new window
notes: Standard username is always 'admin'

section: Logging in to the Argo CD instance by using the Argo CD admin account
evidence: To log in with your OpenShift Container Platform credentials, ensure you are a user of the cluster-admins group and then select the LOG IN VIA OPENSHIFT option
notes: Alternative SSO authentication method

section: Logging in to the Argo CD instance by using the Argo CD admin account
evidence: To be a user of the cluster-admins group, use the oc adm groups new cluster-admins <user> command
notes: Prerequisite for OpenShift SSO login

task: Select LOG IN VIA OPENSHIFT option
section: Logging in to the Argo CD instance by using the Argo CD admin account
evidence: select the LOG IN VIA OPENSHIFT option in the Argo CD user interface
notes: Uses OpenShift OAuth for authentication

section: Installing the GitOps CLI
evidence: For Linux distributions, you can download the GitOps argocd CLI as a tar.gz archive
notes: Technology Preview feature for command-line GitOps management

section: Installing the OpenShift GitOps CLI on Linux
evidence: Download the latest version of the CLI tool from the content gateway for your operating system and architecture
notes: Supports multiple architectures: x86_64, s390x, ppc64le, arm64

section: Installing the OpenShift GitOps CLI on Linux
evidence: Download the latest version of the CLI tool from the content gateway
notes: Options: argocd-linux-amd64.tar.gz, argocd-linux-s390x.tar.gz, argocd-linux-ppc64le.tar.gz, argocd-linux-arm64.tar.gz

section: Installing the OpenShift GitOps CLI on Linux
evidence: tar xvzf <file>
notes: Extracts argocd binary

section: Installing the OpenShift GitOps CLI on Linux
evidence: Move the binary to a directory on your PATH environment variable
notes: Requires sudo for /usr/local/bin

section: Installing the OpenShift GitOps CLI on Linux
evidence: sudo mv argocd /usr/local/bin/argocd
notes: Standard location for user-installed binaries

section: Installing the OpenShift GitOps CLI on Linux
evidence: sudo chmod +x /usr/local/bin/argocd
notes: Sets execute permissions

section: Installing the OpenShift GitOps CLI on Linux
evidence: After you install the GitOps argocd CLI, verify that it is available
notes: Checks version and build information

section: Installing the OpenShift GitOps CLI on Linux
evidence: argocd version --client
notes: Shows version, build date, commit, platform, and ExtraBuildInfo with openshift-gitops-version

section: Installing the GitOps CLI
evidence: For Red Hat Enterprise Linux version 8 or later, you can install the GitOps argocd CLI as an RPM by using a package manager
notes: Preferred method for RHEL; enables automatic updates via dnf upgrade

user_story: Register system and enable GitOps repositories
section: Installing the OpenShift GitOps CLI on Linux using an RPM
evidence: Register with Red Hat Subscription Manager by running the following command
notes: Requires active OpenShift subscription and sudo privileges

user_story: Register system and enable GitOps repositories
section: Installing the OpenShift GitOps CLI on Linux using an RPM
evidence: subscription-manager register
notes: Registers system with Red Hat

user_story: Register system and enable GitOps repositories
section: Installing the OpenShift GitOps CLI on Linux using an RPM
evidence: subscription-manager refresh
notes: Pulls latest subscription data

user_story: Register system and enable GitOps repositories
section: Installing the OpenShift GitOps CLI on Linux using an RPM
evidence: subscription-manager list --available --matches '*gitops*' ... subscription-manager attach --pool=<pool_id>
notes: Locates pool ID and attaches subscription

user_story: Register system and enable GitOps repositories
section: Installing the OpenShift GitOps CLI on Linux using an RPM
evidence: subscription-manager repos --enable="gitops-<gitops_version>-for-rhel-<rhel_version>-<arch>-rpms"
notes: Supports x86_64, s390x, ppc64le, aarch64; example: gitops-1.19-for-rhel-8-x86_64-rpms

section: Installing the OpenShift GitOps CLI on Linux using an RPM
evidence: Install the openshift-gitops-argocd-cli package
notes: Package managed by yum/dnf for automatic updates

section: Installing the OpenShift GitOps CLI on Linux using an RPM
evidence: yum install openshift-gitops-argocd-cli
notes: Can also use dnf install

section: Installing the OpenShift GitOps CLI on Linux using an RPM
evidence: argocd version --client
notes: Shows ExtraBuildInfo with openshift-gitops-version
